notes: The doctrinally load-bearing terms are mahā-rathāḥ (the great chariot-warriors / competent peers), bahu-mataḥ (highly esteemed), bhayāt (out of fear), and lāghavam (lightness / smallness). The engineering layer preserves all four. The mahā-rathāḥ is correctly read as the technically-competent peer-class (not the broader market), preserving Shankara's reading that the verdict carries weight precisely because the witnesses are competent to judge. The bahu-mataḥ to lāghavam phase-change is preserved as a structural update — 'bahu-mataḥ to lāghavam: standing earned through years of shipped open-source has become lightness.' The bhayāt-default is preserved with the Shankara-Vishvanatha-Ramanuja convergence: 'the peer-default for why does a senior leave a system mid-cutover is fear of finishing it. Her stated narrative has no path into that default.' The 'vanity-management' misreading is explicitly excluded. PASS.
